ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "code" TEXT /* ISO code for the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* Continent where the country is located */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region within the continent */,
  "area_km2" REAL /* Total area of the country in square kilometers */,
  "population_estimate" INTEGER /* Estimated population of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "religions" (
  "religion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each religion */,
  "religion_name" TEXT /* Name of the religion (e.g., Christianity, Islam) */,
  "parent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent religion (if applicable) */,
  "religion_type" TEXT /* Type of the religion (e.g., monotheistic, polytheistic) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the religion */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("religion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "denominations" (
  "denomination_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each denomination */,
  "religion_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent religion */,
  "denomination_name" TEXT /* Name of the denomination */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the denomination */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("denomination_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the source */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Publisher of the source */,
  "publication_year" INTEGER /* Year of publication */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the data is collected */,
  "religion_id" INTEGER /* ID of the religion */,
  "denomination_id" INTEGER /* ID of the denomination */,
  "adherents" INTEGER /* Number of adherents */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "percent" REAL /* Percentage of adherents within the country */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_religion_id FOREIGN KEY ("religion_id") REFERENCES religions ("religion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_denomination_id FOREIGN KEY ("denomination_id") REFERENCES denominations ("denomination_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statistics" (
  "statistic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the data is collected */,
  "total_adherents" INTEGER /* Total number of adherents of all religions */,
  "total_population" INTEGER /* Total population of the country */,
  "total_percent" REAL /* Total percentage of adherents within the country */,
  "average_age" REAL /* Average age of the population */,
  "urbanization_rate" REAL /* Urbanization rate of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("statistic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_statistics_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, data analyst, admin) */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* Affiliation of the user (e.g., university, research institution) */,
  "research_interests" TEXT /* Research interests of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* ID of the demographic being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data was accessed */,
  "query_parameters" TEXT /* Query parameters used to filter the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_demographic_id FOREIGN KEY ("demographic_id") REFERENCES demographics ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
